items.area: Visibility gap: category managers lack end-to-end S2P visibility, tail spend bypasses oversight, no downstream PO/invoice/payment tracking
items.quote: Kraft Heinz category managers lack end-to-end visibility into their portfolios. Spend below the $1M threshold bypasses category oversight, creating blind spots... no downstream visibility into POs, invoices, or payments after award.

items.area: Analytics gap: spend analytics tooling removed, current replacement unusable, GCC dependency for reports (slow, manual Excel, low confidence)
items.quote: Spend analytics tooling was removed and the current replacement is not usable, forcing reliance on the Global Capability Center for basic reports. This results in slow turnaround, manual Excel work, limited confidence in data.

items.area: Scalability gap: 2026 split into two entities (Global Taste Elevation, North America Grocery) will expose fragmented tools and manual reporting as unscalable, risking cost control and execution accountability
items.quote: Kraft Heinz is preparing for a 2026 split into two independent businesses... the lack of visibility, fragmented tools, and manual reporting model will not scale and creates risk to cost control, supplier rationalization, and execution accountability.

metrics.narrative: The business problem is articulated in Salesforce but never quantified on a buyer call. Customer_Business_Problem__c states 'Kraft Heinz category managers lack end-to-end visibility into their portfolios. Spend below the $1M threshold bypasses category oversight, creating blind spots. Spend analytics tooling was removed and the current replacement is not usable, forcing reliance on the Global Capability Center for basic reports. This results in slow turnaround, manual Excel work, limited confidence in data, and no downstream visibility into POs, invoices, or payments after award.' Compelling_Event__c adds '2026 split into two independent businesses, Global Taste Elevation and North America Grocery... lack of visibility, fragmented tools, and manual reporting model will not scale and creates risk to cost control, supplier rationalization, and execution accountability.' However, zero buyer calls in 2026 means no prospect has verbalized savings targets, headcount impact, cycle-time reduction, error-rate improvement, or ROI expectation. No quantified value case exists. The pain is real but unanchored to a named buyer sponsor and unmeasured. Without a senior pain owner stating a dollar or time metric, urgency and executive buy-in are at risk.

champion.narrative: No proven champion. Mario Rodriguez (Senior Procurement Professional, mario.rodriguez@kraftheinz.com) is the single OpportunityContactRole, but he declined the 18 Feb meeting and accepted the 26 Mar meeting (which was then cancelled). He has not replied to the 12 Jun Horizon invite. Engagement is passive/cooling. Iqra Shakir (iqra.shakir@kraftheinz.com, referenced in tasks and Next_Step_History__c as an early influencer) accepted the 18 Feb meeting but the call was silent/not recorded, and declined the 26 Mar meeting. She also received the 12 Jun Horizon invite but no reply. Stefanie Fink (Head of Global Digital Procurement, stefanie.fink@kraftheinz.com) replied 27 Apr 2026 with a polite but deferring tone: '2026 digital pipeline is already set... will review 2027 priorities in late Q3.' She accepted Mario Castro's LinkedIn request (Arthur completed the connection 26 Jun per Next_Step__c) but has not granted a meeting. Arthur's Next_Step__c 26 Jun notes 'seems Mario was ghosted?' signaling the practitioner-level thread is cold. Kristyn O'Grady (referenced in Next_Step_History__c 14 Apr 2026 and tasks as the referral source, 'Arthur connected with Kristyn O'Grady at NAPES and she recommended we engage the relevant stakeholder') is positioned as a coach/referral anchor but is not a Kraft Heinz employee (likely a peer/industry contact). Champion strength is developing at best: Stefanie Fink is accessible via LinkedIn and replied once, but she has not advocated, introduced us to the DM/EB, or driven the agenda. Mario Rodriguez and Iqra Shakir are disengaged. No evidence of internal advocacy (shared docs, competitive pushback, agenda-driving). The move is to re-engage Stefanie Fink when her late-Q3 2026 review begins, ask her to champion Zycus internally by introducing us to the DM/EB and the buying committee for each future entity, and to test her willingness to advocate (will she share our POV with peers? will she push back on competitors for us?).

sources: SF Competitors__c (iValua, SAP Ariba, Sievo, DocuSign)
Zero calls or Next Steps mentioning competitive dynamics
competition.narrative: Four competitors listed in Competitors__c (iValua, SAP Ariba, Sievo, DocuSign), set at opp creation 02 Feb 2026, but no recent call, Next Step, or Task corroborates active competitive evaluation, shortlisting, or head-to-head comparison. The competitive landscape is stale/dormant. Why_not_Zycus__c and Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c state: 'Zycus provides a single AI-driven orchestration layer that gives category managers real-time visibility across intake, sourcing, contracts, POs, invoices, and payments, including tail spend. Self-service analytics remove dependency on the GCC for reporting, while centralized intake via the relevant stakeholder restores upstream control. This enables Kraft Heinz to support both future entities with consistent visibility, stronger category ownership, and scalable operating discipline.' This is the Zycus wedge (AI orchestration, self-service analytics, Teams intake, dual-entity support) but it has not been tested against a named competitor on a buyer call. Stefanie Fink's 27 Apr email does not mention competitors. Next_Step_History__c does not reference competitive dynamics. The lack of buyer engagement means we do not know if Kraft Heinz is sole-sourcing, running a bake-off, or simply not evaluating yet. The move is to ask Stefanie Fink (when the late-Q3 review begins) who else they are considering, what they like about each, and to position the Merlin/Agentic AI differentiation (ANA, Merlin Intake) as a Phase 2 centerpiece if the split creates urgency for AI-driven orchestration.

sources: Inferred from Next_Step_History__c and task descriptions (initial discovery with Mario Rodriguez, Iqra Shakir)
identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is articulated in Salesforce but never confirmed by a buyer on a call. Customer_Business_Problem__c: 'Kraft Heinz category managers lack end-to-end visibility into their portfolios. Spend below the $1M threshold bypasses category oversight, creating blind spots. Spend analytics tooling was removed and the current replacement is not usable, forcing reliance on the Global Capability Center for basic reports. This results in slow turnaround, manual Excel work, limited confidence in data, and no downstream visibility into POs, invoices, or payments after award.' Compelling_Event__c: 'Kraft Heinz is preparing for a 2026 split into two independent businesses, Global Taste Elevation and North America Grocery. Leadership views this as an opportunity to redesign procurement processes rather than replicate current inefficiencies. With future procurement teams expected to operate in parallel, the lack of visibility, fragmented tools, and manual reporting model will not scale and creates risk to cost control, supplier rationalization, and execution accountability.' The pain is confirmed (these fields were populated early, likely from initial discovery with Mario Rodriguez or Iqra Shakir). However, the pain owner is not named. Is it Stefanie Fink? A VP Procurement for each future entity? The CFO? Without a named senior sponsor who owns the pain and has budget authority, the pain is documented but not activated. The move is to ask the pain owner to quantify the impact (dollars wasted on manual reporting, supplier leakage below $1M, risk to the split execution) and to own the business case.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics unknown. Current_Contract_Expiration__c is null (no forcing function from an expiring incumbent contract). Next_Step_History__c 18 Feb 2026 states 'Mario Rodriguez to route NDA to Alexa for signature... Legal approved signing Kraft Heinz NDA as-is,' indicating an NDA was in flight in Feb 2026, but no evidence it was executed or that it unblocked deeper discovery (the 18 Feb and 26 Mar meetings never materialized as real calls). No discussion of MSA/SOW, legal review, procurement channel (direct vs partner-mediated), or InfoSec requirements. No SI (systems integrator) or partner role articulated. For a $250K S2P deal at a F500 company with ERP/systems integration needs, contracting is typically procurement-led with legal and InfoSec gates, but those gates are not mapped. The move is to ask Stefanie Fink (or the eventual DM/EB) to outline the contracting steps (MSA, SOW, legal redlines, InfoSec review, ERP integration scoping) and timeline, and to confirm whether a partner (e.g. a Big 4 or boutique integrator) will be involved in scoping or implementation.
